expectations.text: registers-with-Alpine-data: Uses Alpine.data('name', () => ({...})) to register the component (CSP requirement, replaces inline x-data object)
expectations.evidence: matched: ...r the component up front via `Alpine.data('name', () => ({...}))` on `a...

expectations.text: fires-on-alpine-init: Registers the component on the alpine:init event (the canonical CSP-safe registration timing)
x-data-references-name-only: Uses x-data="<name>" referencing a registered component, not an inline object
no-inline-negation: Does NOT use `accepted = !accepted` or any inline negation (forbidden under strict CSP)
uses-method-reference-for-click: @click is a method reference (no parens, no inline expression) like `@click="toggle"`
mentions-createBooleanObject-or-toggle-method: Either uses hyva.createBooleanObject for the boolean or defines a toggle() method on the component
mentions-CSP-context: Mentions CSP, PCI-DSS, or strict CSP — confirms the answer addresses the CSP requirement
